【Day 19】From ASCII to Unicode — A Comprehensive Text Encoding Upgrade
Code Review of My Youth - From the BIOS Beep to Software Engineering
An in-depth look at the differences between ASCII, EASCII, and Unicode, explaining character sets and encoding principles — a full walkthrough of UTF-8 and the root causes of garbled text.
JavaScript Number Formatting: The Easiest Way (Thousands Separators and Currency)

How do you quickly format a number into a thousands separator or currency format in JavaScript? This post rounds up three approaches — toLocaleString, regular expressions, and Intl.NumberFormat — and compares the old-school way with the easiest modern way to get it done.
【Day 18】A Is 65?! I Finally Get What ASCII and Bits Actually Are

What is ASCII? Why does uppercase A equal 65? This post starts with a mysterious string of numbers a senior sent from an internet café and walks through ASCII character encoding, bits, and binary conversion, with a hands-on JavaScript Console demo.
【Day 17】A Language Even Simpler Than Me? The Computer World Only Has 0 and 1!
Think colors and text are complex? In a computer's eyes, it's only 0 and 1. Using game memories and small exercises, this post walks you through the magic of binary.
【Day 16】Playing Games in Class Made Me Miss What the OSI 7-Layer Model Is

Starting from pressing Enter in the browser, we follow data from the application layer down and back up to the screen. The functions of each layer along with common protocols and TCP/IP mappings are organized into one list.
【Day 15】Slow Computer, But I Don't Want to Reinstall Windows Again!

I don't want to keep reinstalling Windows just because the computer slows down. This post walks through the common fixes — managing startup programs, cleaning Temp files (with a simple .bat script), HDD defrag vs SSDs — plus the time I accidentally deleted System32.
【Day 14】I Installed Cracked Software and Broke My Computer Again

A lag incident from cracked Office. Covers hosts, Keygen / Patch bypass principles and risks, plus quick reference on free / shareware / proprietary / free software / open-source licensing differences — a reference for software install decisions.
【Day 13】I Thought I Was in The Matrix: Opening CMD for the First Time

A network-troubleshooting story from my first time opening CMD — basic uses of ping, ipconfig, tracert, netstat with macOS equivalents, plus terminal easter eggs and Homebrew (sl, cowsay, fortune, cmatrix). A Lag incident that strings together the practicality and fun of the command line.
【Day 12】My First PC Upgrade: From No-More-Cafés Punishment to Learning HDDs, SSDs, and RAM

From being banned from internet cafés to upgrading a hand-me-down computer: learning HDD/SSD differences and interfaces, TBW longevity concepts, and RAM capacity and frequency basics. Includes capacity recommendations by use case as upgrade-planning reference.
【Day 11】Virus! My First Time Reinstalling the System

Computer infected and homepage hijacked to hao123? Through a real story, this post covers infection vectors, backups, USB install media, BIOS / UEFI setup, the reinstall flow, and a useful software list — for system recovery and ongoing maintenance.
